body.body:
Sub-slice 1 of T-experiment-primitive (#456). This is the smallest tractable first step — just the data model. The scheduler, outcome feedback, cockpit integration, and CP-check sub-slices all consume this.

## What ships

A new module wild_igor/igor/cognition/experiment.py with dataclasses:

- **Hypothesis** — structured proposition (e.g. 'input X should produce outcome Y', 'habit H will fire for trigger T', 'source S is trustworthy on topic K'). Fields: statement, source, confidence, cp_constraints.
- **Probe** — concrete action to exercise the hypothesis. Fields: kind (memory_query | tool_call | habit_dryrun | channel_send | db_query | sim_turn), target, payload, expected_shape.
- **Observation** — what happened. Fields: outcome (str enum: match | mismatch | partial | inconclusive), data (dict), timestamp, cost.
- **Update** — what changes in Igor's engrams as a result. Fields: trail_edge_changes (list), memory_accretions (list), inhibitor_weight_deltas (dict), goal_state_transitions (list), reason.
- **Experiment** — the full quartet: id + hypothesis + probe + observation + update + status. Status enum: proposed | running | observed | updated | aborted.

## Integration with T-decision-blob-schema (already shipped)

The decision_blob module already has a `ProposedExperiment` dataclass with fields (hypothesis, probe, expected_observation, cost_estimate) — that's the seed. The full Experiment dataclass in this ticket is the complete lifecycle around that seed: what happens after the experiment runs.

ProposedExperiment becomes the 'draft' state; Experiment is the full object after scheduling. Clean relationship:
```
ProposedExperiment (in decision blob) → Experiment.proposed → Experiment.running → Experiment.observed → Experiment.updated
```

## Axiomatic grounding

- CP1: Hypothesis.confidence is a field, never assumed. Observations can be 'inconclusive' without failing.
- CP2: failed Observations trigger Update objects that feed learning. Failure is a state, not an error.
- CP3: Hypothesis must have a causal statement. Probe must specify what it expects. Observation must relate back to the hypothesis.
- CP6: no experiment can update Igor's state without passing through the Update layer — no direct mutation from hypothesis-bearing sources.

## Tests

~15 tests covering:
- Construction of each dataclass with required fields
- Enum coercion (status, outcome, probe kind)
- Experiment lifecycle state transitions (proposed → running → observed → updated) with validation of allowed transitions
- Serialization roundtrip (dict + JSON)
- Integration: Experiment.from_proposed_experiment(ProposedExperiment) cross-module constructor
- CP validation fields populate correctly

## Out of scope

- Scheduler (T-experiment-primitive-scheduler sub-slice)
- Outcome feedback into engrams (T-experiment-primitive-outcome-feedback sub-slice)
- Cockpit integration (T-experiment-primitive-cockpit-integration sub-slice)
- Audit CP-check (T-experiment-primitive-cp-check sub-slice)
- Actually running experiments (this is just the data model)

## Dependencies

- Parent: T-experiment-primitive (#456)
- Builds on: T-decision-blob-schema (shipped a0d224b9, provides ProposedExperiment seed)

## Provenance

Filed during the 2000-2100 solo sprint window as a natural follow-on to T-decision-blob-schema — same shape, same approach, small scope, unblocks downstream.
body.title: Experiment primitive: data model (hypothesis + probe + observation + update)
body.result: 64e1d472 — full experiment dataclass quartet (Hypothesis + Probe + Observation + Update) + Experiment lifecycle state machine (PROPOSED → RUNNING → OBSERVED → UPDATED + ABORTED branch) + from_proposed() bridge from decision_blob.ProposedExperiment. CP-axiomatic grounding in every dataclass docstring. 26 tests, 2256 pass. Unblocks T-experiment-primitive sub-slices: scheduler, outcome-feedback, cockpit-integration, cp-check.
